divisions.name: Corporate & Other
divisions.description: Corporate functions including finance, legal, IT, human resources, and other shared services supporting Enterprise operations.

divisions.name: Regional Utility
divisions.description: Rate-regulated electric utility serving Florida. One of the largest electric utilities in the U.S. with 35,052 MW net generating capacity.

divisions.name: Renewable Energy Division
divisions.description: World's largest generator of renewable energy from wind and solar. Leading battery storage provider with 33,410 MW net generating capacity.

teams.name: Wind Development Team
teams.description: Onshore and offshore wind project development

teams.name: Storage Solutions Team
teams.description: Battery storage and grid services development

teams.name: Google Partnership Team
teams.description: Google Cloud data center dedicated power

projects.name: Enterprise Cybersecurity & NERC CIP Compliance
projects.description: Enterprise cybersecurity program ensuring NERC CIP compliance for critical infrastructure, implementing zero-trust architecture, and protecting against evolving threats.
projects.okr_key_result: Complete NERC CIP v7 compliance for all assets
projects.ai_recommendation: Pre-audit identified 3 medium-severity gaps in substation access controls. Remediation plan required before formal audit.

projects.name: Enterprise Enterprise Data Platform
projects.description: Unified enterprise data platform consolidating data from Regional Utility, Renewables Division, and corporate systems. Enables AI/ML capabilities, advanced analytics, and regulatory reporting across Enterprise.
projects.okr_objective: Unify enterprise data across all business units
projects.okr_key_result: Complete data lake migration for 100% of assets
projects.ai_recommendation: Migration ahead of schedule - 85% complete. Early delivery bonus of $500K achievable. Prioritize remaining migration tasks.

projects.name: Enterprise ESG & Sustainability Reporting Platform
projects.description: Integrated ESG reporting platform for SEC climate disclosures, sustainability reporting, and stakeholder communications. Supports Enterprise's leadership in clean energy transition.

projects.name: NEET Transmission Expansion Program
projects.description: Strategic transmission development through NEET to enable renewable integration and improve grid reliability. Focus on competitive transmission projects in MISO, SPP, and PJM.

projects.name: Regional Utility Advanced Metering Infrastructure 2.0
projects.description: Next-generation smart meter deployment with enhanced two-way communication, real-time usage analytics, and integration with grid automation. Upgrading 5.8 million customer endpoints.
projects.ai_recommendation: Pilot areas showing 15% faster outage detection - exceeding targets. Strong candidate for accelerated rollout to remaining accounts.

projects.name: Regional Utility Customer Digital Experience Platform
projects.description: Next-generation customer portal and mobile app with AI-powered support, real-time outage tracking, and self-service capabilities. Target: 90% digital adoption and 50% reduction in call center volume.
projects.okr_key_result: Launch mobile app 2.0 with real-time usage
projects.ai_recommendation: User testing feedback positive. Mobile app NPS at 72 - above industry benchmark. Consider accelerating iOS release.

projects.name: Regional Utility Grid Modernization & Automation
projects.description: AI-powered grid management system with automated switching, real-time load balancing, and predictive outage prevention. Part of Regional Utility's $8.2B capital investment program targeting 99.98% reliability.
projects.ai_recommendation: High priority - legacy SCADA integration delays detected. Recommend cloud migration to reduce integration complexity by 40%. Schedule API performance review immediately.

projects.name: Regional Utility Storm Protection Plan - Hurricane Hardening
projects.description: 10-year storm protection plan accelerating undergrounding of distribution lines, hardening poles, and installing smart switches. Response to Hurricane Milton and Helene impacts in 2024.
projects.okr_objective: Complete coastal zone hardening before hurricane season
projects.ai_recommendation: Only 8 weeks until June 1 hurricane season. Add weekend shifts to accelerate coastal zone completion. Bulk material procurement could save $2.1M.

projects.name: Regional Utility 2025 Rate Case Support System
projects.description: Comprehensive analytics and documentation platform supporting Regional Utility's rate case filing with Florida PSC. Automates cost-of-service studies, rate design modeling, and regulatory reporting.

projects.name: Regional Utility Solar Base Rate Adjustment (SoBRA) Expansion
projects.description: Expansion of Regional Utility's solar generation capacity under the SoBRA mechanism approved by Florida PSC. Adding 1.8 GW of solar capacity across Florida with battery storage co-location.
projects.ai_recommendation: Land acquisition 92% complete - ahead of schedule. Expedite permitting for sites 12-15 to capture $45M ITC benefit before phase-out.

projects.name: Renewables Division Battery Energy Storage Systems
projects.description: Large-scale battery storage deployment for grid services, renewable firming, and energy arbitrage. Target 3 GWh deployment by 2026 across key markets.
projects.ai_recommendation: ERCOT summer capacity prices 18% higher than forecast. Lock in rates early for $8M revenue improvement. CATL battery supply secured for Q3-Q4.

projects.name: Renewables Division Development Pipeline Management System
projects.description: Digital platform for managing Renewables Division's 300+ GW renewable development pipeline from origination through construction. Integrates land, permitting, interconnection, and offtake tracking.

projects.name: Duane Arnold Nuclear Restart Assessment
projects.description: Feasibility study and preliminary engineering for potential restart of Duane Arnold Energy Center in Iowa. Exploring commercial arrangements and regulatory pathway.

projects.name: Renewables Division Green Hydrogen Pilot Program
projects.description: Pilot-scale green hydrogen production using dedicated renewable energy. Targeting industrial customers and exploring hydrogen hub opportunities under DOE programs.
projects.ai_recommendation: IRA hydrogen production tax credit guidance favorable. Accelerate electrolyzer procurement to capture 2024 pricing before demand spike.

projects.name: Renewables Division PPA Portfolio Analytics Platform
projects.description: Advanced analytics platform for managing Renewables Division's $20B+ PPA portfolio. Real-time pricing, contract optimization, and risk management with AI-powered insights.

projects.name: Renewables Division Wind Fleet Repowering Program
projects.description: Systematic repowering of aging wind assets with modern turbines to extend asset life, increase capacity, and qualify for new tax credits. Targeting 2+ GW of repowered capacity by 2027.

projects.name: Renewables Division Utility-Scale Solar Development
projects.description: Development of utility-scale solar projects across ERCOT, CAISO, and PJM markets. Part of Renewables Division's industry-leading renewable development pipeline with 2.5 GW annual deployment target.
projects.ai_recommendation: New tariff announcement may increase panel costs 12%. Evaluate alternative suppliers urgently. Land acquisition proceeding on schedule.

projects.name: Renewables Division Wind Portfolio Expansion 2024-2027
projects.description: Expansion of Renewables Division's wind generation portfolio as part of the 36.5-46.5 GW renewable buildout plan 2024-2027. Focus on Texas ERCOT, SPP, and MISO markets with repowering of existing assets.
projects.okr_objective: Add 8.5 GW new wind capacity by 2027
projects.okr_key_result: Commission 12 new wind farms across 6 states
projects.ai_recommendation: Vestas shipment showing 2-week delay for Arecleoch site. Executive escalation recommended. Wind resource 8% above P50 forecast - NPV improvement opportunity.

projects.name: Google Cloud Data Center Partnership
projects.description: On December 8, 2025, Enterprise and Google Cloud announced a landmark strategic energy and technology partnership. Google has already contracted roughly 3.5 GW of energy capacity from Enterprise, with plans to develop 15 GW of new power generation for data center hubs by 2035. The partnership includes joint development of multiple GW-scale data center campuses across the U.S., with three campuses currently in development. Each campus integrates dedicated power generation including solar, battery storage, and natural gas. The technology collaboration gives Enterprise access to Google's AI models including TimesFM 2.5 for time-series forecasting and WeatherNext 2 for weather prediction. Enterprise has secured 4 GW of combined-cycle gas turbine slots from GE Vernova to support this initiative. The partnership addresses data center power demand projected to represent 43% of U.S. power demand growth through 2032.
projects.expected_roi: $2.8B projected revenue from data center power sales
projects.okr_objective: Secure 4.6 GW capacity for hyperscale data centers
projects.ai_recommendation: Favorable ERCOT queue position secured - 18 month advantage. GE Vernova turbine slots confirmed. Recommend filing additional Phase 2 applications.

projects.name: Meta Clean Energy Partnership
projects.description: Announced December 8, 2025, Enterprise signed a significant clean energy partnership with Meta for approximately 2.5 GW of capacity. The deal includes 11 power purchase agreements (PPAs) and 2 energy storage agreements (ESAs) deployed across ERCOT, SPP, MISO, and New Mexico markets. Four integrated solar and battery storage projects are being developed in New Mexico specifically for Meta data centers. Projects are scheduled to come online between 2026 and 2028, adding to nearly 500 MW of projects Meta previously contracted with Enterprise. This partnership supports Meta's 100% renewable energy commitment for its data center operations and represents one of the largest corporate renewable energy deals in the industry.

projects.name: Duane Arnold Nuclear Restart
projects.description: Enterprise is evaluating the restart of the 615 MW Duane Arnold Energy Center in Iowa, which was prematurely shut down in 2020. Google has signed a 25-year power purchase agreement to buy power from the plant if Enterprise proceeds with the restart. The nuclear facility would provide zero-carbon baseload power essential for AI data center operations that require 24/7 reliable electricity. Expected return to service by 2029 pending NRC regulatory approval. This follows a broader industry trend including the Palisades restart in Michigan and Constellation's Three Mile Island Unit 1 restart for Microsoft. The project addresses growing demand for 'firm' carbon-free power that renewables alone cannot provide, with nuclear operating at 90%+ capacity factors compared to 25-35% for solar and wind.

projects.name: 2024 Hurricane Season Recovery & Resilience
projects.description: Regional Utility faced an unprecedented 2024 hurricane season with four major hurricanes causing over 3 million customer outages. Regional Utility filed for $1.2 billion in cost recovery from the Florida Public Service Commission, which was approved via temporary rate increases of approximately $12/month per customer. The storm reserve fund was completely depleted, requiring a $150 million replenishment request. Regional Utility accounts for over 50% of Enterprise's earnings, making hurricane exposure a material risk to the company. This program accelerates infrastructure hardening including pole replacement, undergrounding critical feeders, and installation of smart switches for faster restoration. The 2024 season demonstrated the ongoing climate risk to Florida operations and the need for continued resilience investments ahead of future hurricane seasons.
projects.expected_roi: $180M avoided outage costs from accelerated hardening
projects.okr_objective: Restore power to 100% of customers within 10 days
projects.okr_key_result: Complete storm damage assessment and repairs
projects.ai_recommendation: PSC cost recovery filing due in 45 days. Documentation 78% complete. Assign dedicated team to complete filing requirements.

projects.name: IRA Tax Credit Defense & Policy Strategy
projects.description: With the Trump administration and congressional Republicans targeting IRA repeal to offset TCJA extension costs, Enterprise has committed to defending clean energy tax incentives. IRA green energy tax credits are projected to be worth $1.16 trillion (2025-2034) across the industry, and tax credits can represent 30-50% of project economics for renewable developments. On January 20, 2025, a Trump executive order halted new wind energy permits. While business-facing credits like 45Q carbon capture and 45V hydrogen have bipartisan support and are likely safe, consumer credits are more vulnerable. Enterprise's strategy includes demonstrating that renewables remain competitive even without subsidies in most regions, while diversifying to natural gas partnerships and nuclear to hedge policy risk. Full repeal is unlikely, but partial rollback is possible via budget reconciliation.
projects.expected_roi: $2.4B value preservation if IRA credits maintained

projects.name: ExxonMobil Natural Gas + CCUS Partnership
projects.description: Enterprise announced a partnership with ExxonMobil to develop a 1.2 GW natural gas plant with integrated carbon capture, utilization and storage (CCUS) technology. The project addresses the critical need for 24/7 'firm' power for AI data centers while maintaining decarbonization commitments required by hyperscaler customers like Google and Meta. The facility will use combined-cycle gas technology with ExxonMobil's proven CCUS capturing 90%+ of CO2 emissions for permanent sequestration. The project qualifies for 45Q carbon capture tax credits which have bipartisan support and are expected to survive potential IRA modifications. This represents Enterprise's 'all-of-the-above' energy strategy, combining renewables, storage, natural gas with carbon capture, and nuclear to meet growing AI infrastructure power demands.

projects.name: AI Grid Management Platform
projects.description: As part of the December 2025 Google Cloud partnership, Enterprise gained access to Google's advanced AI models including TimesFM 2.5 for time-series forecasting, WeatherNext 2 for weather prediction, and security-constrained power flow modeling. The first commercial AI grid management product is expected to launch via Google Cloud Marketplace by mid-2026. The platform enables AI-powered field operations including predictive maintenance 30+ days in advance, supply chain optimization, and weather response automation. For Regional Utility hurricane operations, integration with WeatherNext 2 enables improved storm preparation, resource positioning, and restoration optimization. This product represents a potential new revenue stream by licensing Enterprise's operational expertise combined with Google AI to other utilities facing similar grid management challenges.
